title: Verifying GMP Certificates and EudraGMDP
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: A senior QP's guide to GMP certificate verification using EudraGMDP and MHRA: scope, validity, non-compliance checks and a defensible QMS workflow.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury pharmaceutical banner in a cool teal and cyan palette over deep navy, with a subtle green accent. A close, shallow-depth-of-field shot of a quality professional's hands at a sleek glass desk, cross-referencing a crisp official certificate document against a softly glowing tablet screen showing an abstract database interface; out-of-focus vials and a laptop recede into a calm, modern laboratory office bathed in soft natural side light. Clean reflective surfaces, premium and authoritative mood, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: Is a GMP certificate emailed by a supplier sufficient evidence for qualification?
faqs.a: No. A supplier-provided PDF only shows what the supplier chooses to share and may be expired, withdrawn or out of scope. You should independently confirm the entry against EudraGMDP or the MHRA register and record that check. Under EU GMP Chapter 5 and ICH Q10, the contract giver remains accountable regardless of what the contract acceptor supplies.

faqs.q: What is the difference between EudraGMDP and the MHRA register after Brexit?
faqs.a: EudraGMDP is the EU database of manufacturing and import authorisations, GMP and GDP certificates and statements of non-compliance for EU/EEA sites. Following EU exit, the MHRA maintains equivalent UK data for GB sites and authorisations. Use the source appropriate to where the site is located and where the product is placed on the market, and check both where a supply chain spans the two.

faqs.q: How often should GMP certificate verification be repeated?
faqs.a: Verification is not a one-off onboarding task. Re-confirm at qualification, on contract renewal, when you receive change notifications, on adverse intelligence, and at a fixed periodic interval set by risk. A previously valid certificate can be superseded by a restriction or statement of non-compliance, so periodic re-checks against the source database keep your supplier file defensible.
